patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/448

## Problem (data loss class)
A custom field registered `--type boolean` via `pm schema add-field` is silently never persisted when set through its auto-derived flag (`--customer-impact true` style): create/update succeeds, no warning, field absent from item and changed_fields. Boolean is one of four documented custom-field types; any boolean field (customer_impact, regression, is_blocker...) loses data on every write while reporting success.

## Repro (from GH-448, pm 2026.7.4)
fresh tracker -> `pm schema add-field customer_impact --type boolean --commands create,update` -> `pm create ... --customer-impact true` -> field missing.

## Definition of done
Boolean custom fields persist through create/update (coercion fixed); regression spec across all four field types; silent-drop path converted to hard error for unknown/uncoercible custom values; verified in sandbox from packed tarball.
GH-448: boolean custom field is silently never persisted (data-loss class)

Dogfood GH-448: pm schema add-field --type boolean registers fine but values set via the derived flag are silently dropped on create/update — success reported, data lost. Duplicate check: searched boolean custom field — pm-vhbf/pm-qvdj (closed) shipped the field system; no open item covers the boolean coercion bug.

Boolean custom fields persist via auto-derived flags on create+update; regression spec across all four custom-field types; uncoercible values hard-error instead of silent drop; tarball-sandbox verified
